name: Dual-ODE Dynamical System with Wanting-Liking Dissociation
description: A continuous-time ODE system (Euler-discretized per simulation step) where wanting and liking evolve as coupled but dissociable dynamical variables. Wanting is driven by cue proximity (inverse distance to nearest food) and amplified by a sensitization state that accumulates with each consumption event. Liking drifts toward a hedonic set-point but develops tolerance with repeated consumption. Decision-making uses threshold-based rules: high wanting triggers approach/eat, low wanting triggers exploration. The emergent behavior is the hallmark wanting-liking dissociation: over time, cue-triggered wanting escalates (through sensitization) while hedonic liking per consumption stagnates or declines (through tolerance). Based directly on Robinson & Berridge (1993) and Berridge & Robinson (1998, 2016).

variables.name: wanting_level
variables.description: Current motivational drive / incentive salience intensity. Driven up by cue proximity, amplified by sensitization, decays without cues.

variables.name: liking_level
variables.description: Current hedonic capacity / pleasure sensitivity. Drifts toward baseline set-point but decremented by consumption (tolerance). Dopamine-independent (P3).

variables.name: sensitization_state
variables.description: Accumulated neural sensitization of the wanting system. Grows with each reward encounter, decays very slowly. Amplifies cue-triggered wanting.

variables.name: cue_proximity
variables.description: Intensity of nearest reward-predictive cue. Computed as 1/(1+d_min) where d_min is Manhattan distance to nearest visible food. 0 if no food visible.

variables.name: energy
variables.description: Internal energy level reflecting recent consumption minus basal metabolic cost.

variables.name: experienced_pleasure
variables.description: Actual hedonic experience from eating, computed as l_t * r_t. Can diminish even as wanting grows.

variables.name: last_move_direction
variables.description: The last movement action taken, used for recency avoidance in exploration.

parameters.name: wanting_decay_rate
parameters.source: Natural decay of motivational urge without cues

parameters.name: sensitization_gain_on_wanting
parameters.source: Robinson & Berridge (1993): sensitization amplifies cue-triggered wanting

parameters.name: sensitization_decay_rate
parameters.source: Very slow decay — sensitization is long-lasting (Robinson & Berridge, 2024)

parameters.name: liking_restoration_rate
parameters.source: Hedonic adaptation back to baseline (Berridge & Robinson, 1998)

parameters.name: liking_tolerance_decrement
parameters.source: Hedonic tolerance with repeated consumption (Berridge & Robinson, 2016)

parameters.name: wanting_approach_threshold
parameters.source: Below this, agent defaults to exploration

parameters.name: wanting_eat_threshold
parameters.source: High wanting needed to trigger consumption action

parameters.name: euler_timestep
parameters.source: Discretization step for ODE integration (one simulation tick)

rules.description: Wanting dynamics (Euler-discretized ODE): wanting is driven up by cue proximity c_t, amplified by sensitization S_t via the (1 + a_S * S_t) multiplier, and decays naturally. This formalizes P2 and P4: wanting is cue-triggered and dopamine-amplified.
rules.pseudocode:
w_next = w_t + dt * (a_w * (1 + a_S * S_t) * c_t - b_w * w_t)
w_next = max(w_next, 0.0)

rules.description: Liking dynamics (Euler-discretized ODE): liking drifts back toward the hedonic set-point l_0 but is decremented by each consumption event (tolerance). Critically, liking has NO dependence on sensitization or the wanting variables — this implements P3.
rules.pseudocode:
ate = 1 if r_t > 0 else 0
l_next = l_t + dt * (mu_l * (l_0 - l_t) - delta_l * ate)
l_next = max(l_next, 0.01)  # floor to prevent zero

rules.description: Sensitization dynamics (Euler-discretized ODE): sensitization grows with each reward encounter and decays very slowly. This implements P5: repeated exposure produces persistent neuroplastic amplification of wanting.
rules.pseudocode:
ate = 1 if r_t > 0 else 0
S_next = S_t + dt * (eta_S * ate - lambda_S * S_t)
S_next = clip(S_next, 0.0, S_max)

rules.description: Cue proximity signal: inverse of (1 + Manhattan distance to nearest visible food). If no food is visible, c_t = 0.
Experienced pleasure from eating: actual hedonic experience scales with current liking capacity. Can diminish over time even as wanting escalates — the core wanting-liking dissociation (P5).
Energy dynamics: energy decreases each step by metabolic cost and increases when food is consumed.
rules.pseudocode:
ate = 1 if r_t > 0 else 0
E_next = E_t - E_decay + ate * r_t
E_next = max(E_next, 0.0)

decision_logic.description: Threshold-based deterministic decision rules with tie-breaking. The agent's behavior is governed by the current wanting level w_t, which is a continuous dynamical variable driven by cue proximity and sensitization. Four rules are evaluated in priority order: (A) Eat if on food and wanting is high enough; (D) Stay/hover if on food but wanting hasn't reached eat threshold; (B) Approach nearest food if wanting exceeds approach threshold; (C) Explore randomly if wanting is low. This creates emergent behavioral transitions: early on (low sensitization), the agent explores; over time (high sensitization), it becomes increasingly cue-reactive and approach-dominant.
pseudocode: food_here = any(f for f in food_list if f['x'] == x and f['y'] == y)
d_min = min(abs(f['x'] - x) + abs(f['y'] - y) for f in food_list)
# Update wanting BEFORE decision (so current cue proximity drives this step's behavior)
w_t = w_t + dt * (a_w * (1 + a_S * S_t) * c_t - b_w * w_t)
# Rule A: Eat — on food and wanting is high enough
# Rule D: Stay/hover — on food but wanting building up
if food_here and theta_w <= w_t < theta_e:
# Rule B: Approach — wanting above approach threshold, food visible
nearest = min(food_list, key=lambda f: abs(f['x'] - x) + abs(f['y'] - y))
# Move toward nearest food, preferring axis with larger distance
return Action('stay')  # already at food (shouldn't reach here normally)
# Rule C: Explore — low wanting, random movement with recency avoidance
preferred = [m for m in moves if m != opposites[last_move]]

perception_to_variables.food_sources: perception['resources']['food']  # list of {x, y, type, palatability}
perception_to_variables.food_at_current_cell: any(f for f in perception['resources']['food'] if f['x'] == perception['x'] and f['y'] == perception['y'])
perception_to_variables.nearest_food_distance: min(abs(f['x'] - perception['x']) + abs(f['y'] - perception['y']) for f in food_sources) if food_sources else infinity
perception_to_variables.reward: reward parameter from update(action, reward, new_perception)

env_mapping.reward_source: eat action → ConsumeEffect reward (1.0). The reward r_t from update() drives sensitization growth (R3), liking tolerance (R2), energy gain (R6), and pleasure computation (R5).

expected_behaviors.description: Early exploration phase: with low sensitization, wanting stays below approach threshold most of the time, and the agent explores the grid randomly (Rule C dominates).
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: run first 50 steps with S_t=0 → assert agent uses all 4 movement directions roughly equally, rarely eats even near food

expected_behaviors.description: Transition to approach-dominant behavior: as sensitization accumulates, wanting spikes more strongly near food cues, and the agent increasingly approaches food (Rule B dominates).
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: run 1000 steps → compare fraction of Rule B actions in first 200 vs last 200 steps → assert increase

expected_behaviors.description: Wanting-liking dissociation: wanting peak values increase over time (due to growing S_t amplifying cue reactivity) while liking per consumption event decreases (due to tolerance). The agent pursues food more vigorously but enjoys it less.
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: track max(w_t) per 100-step window and avg(pleasure_t) per eating event → assert wanting trend up, pleasure trend down or flat

expected_behaviors.description: Hovering behavior: when the agent is near food but wanting hasn't reached eat threshold, it stays in place (Rule D), modeling the build-up of craving near reward-predictive cues.
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: observe agent on food cell with theta_w <= w_t < theta_e → assert agent chooses 'stay'

expected_behaviors.description: Sensitization persistence: even after a long period without food, sensitization decays very slowly (lambda_S = 0.002), so when food reappears the agent rapidly returns to high-wanting approach behavior.
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: let agent sensitize (S_t > 2), remove food for 200 steps → assert S_t still > 1.5 → reintroduce food → assert agent immediately approaches

expected_behaviors.description: Liking recovery: after a period without consumption, liking drifts back toward baseline l_0, modeling hedonic adaptation.
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: consume food until l_t < 0.7 → stop eating for 100 steps → assert l_t has increased toward l_0

references: Robinson, T.E. & Berridge, K.C. (1993). The neural basis of drug craving: An incentive-sensitization theory of addiction. Brain Research Reviews, 18(3), 247-291.
Berridge, K.C. & Robinson, T.E. (1998). What is the role of dopamine in reward: hedonic impact, reward learning, or incentive salience? Brain Research Reviews, 28(3), 309-369.
Berridge, K.C. (2007). The debate over dopamine's role in reward: the case for incentive salience. Psychopharmacology, 191(3), 391-431.
Berridge, K.C. & Robinson, T.E. (2016). Liking, Wanting and the Incentive-Sensitization Theory of Addiction. PMC5171207.
Robinson, T.E. & Berridge, K.C. (2024). The Incentive-Sensitization Theory of Addiction: 30 Years On. Annual Review of Psychology.
Redish, A.D. (2004). Addiction as a computational process gone awry. Science, 306(5703), 1944-1947.
